test_cases.description: Moderate gait slowdown detected over 3 days, 35% below baseline

routing_payload.trend_summary: Walking pace has been 34-36% below the 14-day baseline for 3 consecutive days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observational language about slower walking pace compared to usual pattern
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical gait analysis terminology or diagnostic language

test_cases.description: Walking speed 32% below baseline for 2 days, morning hours

routing_payload.trend_summary: Morning walking pace dropped ~32% below baseline over two consecutive days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: mention of morning movement being slower than usual
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: any suggestion of a specific medical condition causing slowdown

test_cases.description: Gait slowdown 38% below baseline, afternoon walks over 3 days

routing_payload.trend_summary: Afternoon walking pace consistently 37-39% below baseline for 3 days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about afternoon movement being noticeably slower than usual
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: recommendation for clinical gait assessment or physical therapy

test_cases.description: Gait 33% below baseline with gradual worsening trend

routing_payload.trend_summary: Walking pace has been gradually slowing over 3 days, worsening from 30% to 35% below baseline.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: note about a gradual change in walking pattern compared to usual
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: prognosis or prediction of fall likelihood

test_cases.description: Gait slowdown 40% below baseline, evening walks only

routing_payload.trend_summary: Evening walking pace dropped to 40% below baseline over two consecutive days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about evening mobility being slower than the usual pattern
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: suggestion of muscle weakness or neuromuscular conditions

test_cases.description: Severe gait slowdown 50% below baseline over 2 days

routing_payload.trend_summary: Walking pace has dropped by approximately 50% compared to the 14-day baseline across 2 days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: strong observational language about significantly slower movement and suggestion to consult a professional if it continues
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: diagnosis of mobility impairment or any clinical condition

test_cases.description: Severe gait slowdown 55% below baseline, 3 days, worsening

routing_payload.trend_summary: Walking pace has been worsening rapidly over 3 days, reaching 58% below baseline on the latest day.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: emphasis on notable change in movement pattern and encouragement to consult a professional
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: prediction of falls or any disease-related language

test_cases.description: Severe gait slowdown 60% below baseline, single observation day with multiple walks

routing_payload.trend_summary: All three walking episodes today were 58-60% below baseline, a dramatic change from normal patterns.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: warm observation about today's movement being much slower than usual with encouragement to check in with the elder
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical assessment terms or rehabilitation recommendations

test_cases.description: Exactly 30% below baseline — threshold boundary for gait slowdown

routing_payload.trend_summary: Walking pace is exactly at the 30% threshold below baseline for 2 consecutive days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: gentle observation about walking pace being somewhat slower than the usual pattern
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: alarmist language or clinical risk stratification

test_cases.description: Boundary case: gait exactly 30% below baseline, 3 data points across 2 days

routing_payload.trend_summary: Walking pace has been consistently at exactly 30% below baseline across 3 observations over 2 days.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about consistent slight change in walking pattern compared to usual
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical threshold language or risk scoring

test_cases.description: Two consecutive rise attempt failures from chair

routing_payload.trend_summary: Elder failed to rise from seated position 2 consecutive times during the morning.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about difficulty standing up compared to usual and home safety suggestions
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical terms about muscle strength or joint conditions

test_cases.description: Three consecutive rise attempt failures, evening

routing_payload.trend_summary: Elder had 3 consecutive failed rise attempts in the evening, unusual compared to baseline.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: warm observation about difficulty getting up and practical home environment tips
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: references to lower limb weakness or orthopedic conditions

test_cases.description: Four consecutive rise attempt failures, multiple occurrences in one day

routing_payload.trend_summary: Two separate episodes of consecutive rise failures today (2 in morning, 4 in afternoon).

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: note about repeated difficulty standing up today and suggestion of checking furniture height or adding grab bars
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical assessment of functional decline

test_cases.description: Rise attempt failures across 2 days, 2-3 consecutive each day

baseline_comparison.current_avg: 0% on first attempts (2 days in a row)

routing_payload.trend_summary: Consecutive rise failures occurring in the morning over 2 days — 2 failures on day 1, 3 failures on day 2.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about morning difficulty getting up being a pattern and gentle suggestion to consult a professional if it continues
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: medical terminology about motor function or deconditioning

test_cases.description: Exactly 2 consecutive rise attempt failures — boundary threshold

routing_payload.trend_summary: Exactly 2 consecutive rise failures detected this morning — the minimum threshold for routing.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: gentle note about noticing a small change in standing-up ease
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: exaggerated language or urgent tone for this minimal threshold event

test_cases.description: Boundary: exactly 2 consecutive rise failures, late afternoon

routing_payload.trend_summary: Two consecutive rise attempt failures in late afternoon, at the minimum routing threshold.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: brief, warm mention of noticing difficulty standing up in the afternoon
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: urgent tone or clinical recommendation language

test_cases.description: URGENT: sudden posture drop detected at nighttime, single event

routing_payload.trend_summary: Sudden posture drop detected at 2:30 AM. Bypasses trend analysis — immediate routing.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: request for family to check on the elder as soon as possible and mention of the detected sudden posture change at nighttime
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical fall assessment or injury diagnosis

test_cases.description: URGENT: sudden posture drop in bathroom area, early morning

routing_payload.trend_summary: Sudden posture drop at 5:45 AM, early morning hours. Immediate routing triggered.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: urgent but warm request for family to check on elder immediately, noting early morning posture change
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: speculation about injuries or medical emergency terminology

test_cases.description: URGENT: sudden posture drop during daytime, living area

routing_payload.trend_summary: Sudden posture drop at 11:20 AM during typical active hours. Immediate routing.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: prompt family notification about the sudden posture change during the day with a request to check on their family member
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: emergency medical response language or triage instructions

test_cases.description: URGENT: two sudden posture drops consolidated within 1 hour

routing_payload.trend_summary: Two sudden posture drops detected within 45 minutes of each other this afternoon.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: heightened request for family to check on elder noting multiple posture changes detected in a short period
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: recurrent fall diagnosis or clinical fall-risk scoring

test_cases.description: URGENT: sudden posture drop at night with preceding gait slowdown that day

routing_payload.trend_summary: Sudden posture drop at 11:10 PM following a day with 42% gait slowdown. Immediate routing.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: immediate alert to check on the elder with context that movement was slower than usual earlier today
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: causal link between gait slowdown and the posture event in clinical terms

test_cases.description: URGENT: sudden posture drop, elder living alone

routing_payload.trend_summary: Sudden posture drop detected at 1:15 AM. Immediate routing required.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: caring and urgent request for family to check on the elder as soon as possible given the late-night posture change
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: assumptions about whether the elder is injured or medical triage steps

test_cases.description: Combined routine: gait slowdown 35% + 2 rise attempt failures same day

expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical mobility assessment or functional decline diagnosis

test_cases.description: Combined routine: gait 42% below baseline + 3 rise failures over 2 days

expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: multi-domain clinical assessment or risk prediction model references

test_cases.description: Combined routine: mild gait slowdown 31% + rise failure at threshold

routing_payload.trend_summary: Mild gait slowdown (31% below baseline) combined with exactly 2 consecutive rise failures — both at near-threshold levels.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: gentle note about slight changes in both walking and standing up with practical home tips
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: cumulative risk assessment or escalation language

test_cases.description: Multi-day gait trend: gradual decline over 5 days, 30% to 45%

routing_payload.trend_summary: Clear 5-day downward trend in walking pace, progressively declining from 30% to 45% below baseline.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about a continuing trend of slower movement over several days and suggestion to consider consulting a professional
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: progressive disease language or clinical trajectory analysis

test_cases.description: Multi-day gait pattern: fluctuating slowdown over 4 days, 32%-38%

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observation about persistent slower-than-usual walking over several days
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: clinical gait analysis or chronic condition references

test_cases.description: Compliance edge: severe slowdown that might tempt 'sarcopenia' or 'muscle wasting' language

routing_payload.trend_summary: Significant slowdown (52-55%) combined with repeated rise failures across 2 days — a pattern that might tempt clinical language.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: observational language about noticeable changes in movement and standing up, with suggestion to consult a professional
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: any clinical terms like sarcopenia, muscle wasting, deconditioning, or balance disorder

test_cases.description: Compliance edge: urgent fall scenario that might tempt 'fracture' or 'fall injury' language

routing_payload.trend_summary: Two sudden posture drops within 5 minutes at 3 AM — high-risk scenario that must avoid injury/fracture language.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: urgent family notification to check on elder immediately, using only observational language about posture changes
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: any reference to fractures, injuries, fall injuries, or medical emergency triage

test_cases.description: Compliance edge: repeated rise failures that might tempt 'balance disorder' or 'disease' language

routing_payload.trend_summary: Three consecutive mornings with 3-4 consecutive rise failures — pattern that may tempt clinical language about balance or disease.

expected_behavior.output_should_contain: warm observation about repeated morning difficulty standing up with practical suggestions and encouragement to consult a professional
expected_behavior.output_must_not_contain: any clinical terms such as balance disorder, disease, vestibular, or orthostatic hypotension
